LAW AND MOTION 302, PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION, DEFENDANTS CUMULUS MEDIA INC., CMP SUSQUEHANNA RADIO CORP, BILL BUNGEROTH, LEE HAMMER AND LEW DICKEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CUMULUS MEDIA INC., CUMULUS MEDIA PARTNERS LLC, CUMULUS - SAN FRANCISCO AND CMP SUSQUEHANNA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS FILED IN SUPPORT OF PLAITIFF'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT OPPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO SEAL ALL OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS EXCEPT ITEMS 4 (ATTACHMENT B TO SECOND REQUEST FOR ADMISSION TO LEE HAMMER, EX. G TO THE ROBINSON DECLARATION) AND 5 (ATTACHMENT B TO SECOND REQUEST FOR ADMISSION TO SUSQUEHANNA RADIO CORP., EX. I TO THE ROBINSON DECLARATION). AS TO THE REMAINING DOCUMENTS, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DOCUMENTS CONTAIN COMMERCIALLY SENSITIVE BUSINESS INFORMATION AND/OR CONFIDENTIAL TRADE SECRETS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORDS, THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORDS, A SUBSTANTIALLY PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST IF THE RECORDS ARE NOT SEALED, THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. AS SUCH, SEALING OF THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS IS PROPER PURSUANT TO RULE OF COURT 2.550. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Pltf'S Summary Judgment Opposition; Memo Of Pts And Auth; Decs Of Elizabeht Bulat Turner And Lee Hammer
Off Calendar Sep-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS CUMULUS MEDIA INC., CUMULUS MEDIA PARTNERS LLC, CUMULUS - SAN FRANCISCO AND CMP SUSQUEHANNA CORPORATION'S Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Plaitiff's Summary Judgment Opposition is Granted in part. No opposition filed. The Court finds good cause to seal all of the requested documents except items 4 (Attachment B to Second Request for Admission to Lee Hammer, Ex. G to the Robinson Declaration) and 5 (Attachment B to Second Request for Admission to Susquehanna Radio Corp., Ex. I to the Robinson Declaration). As to the remaining documents, the Court finds that the documents contain commercially sensitive business information and/or confidential trade secrets. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records, the overriding interest supports sealing the records, a substantially probability exists that the overriding interest if the records are not sealed, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. As such, sealing of the confidential materials is proper pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550. No opposition was filed. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Continued To Aug-14-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Take Mental Examination Of Pltf
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. [The Court did not receive objections to its tentative rulings pursuant to SFLR 8.3(D).] The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant CUMULUS MEDIA INC.'S Motion for Leave of Court to Take Mental Examination of Plaintiff is denied without prejudice. CCP sec. 2032.320(d) requires that an order for mental exam specify "person or persons who may perform the exam, as well as the time, place manner, DIAGNOSITC TESTS, PROCEDURES, condition, SCOPE and nature of the examination." (emphasis added). It is not clear from the order presented or the papers what or whether written tests will be administered. If so, they must be identified and who is administering them must be identified. The doctor suggests in his declaration that other tests MIGHT be performed as well. All such tests must be identified in the order as well as the scope of the examination. Therefore, the motion is denied and Defendant has leave to represent a motion and order that satisfies the CCP. Prevailing party to submit a form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
